steps.title: Rewrite the radical
steps.content:
A fifth root is the same as raising to the power 
$$
\frac{1}{5}
$$:
$$
\sqrt[5]{x^8y^8}=(x^8y^8)^{\frac{1}{5}}
$$

steps.title: Combine the factors
steps.content:
Since $x$ and $y$ are positive, the factors can be grouped as a power of $xy$:
$$
x^8y^8=(xy)^8
$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule
steps.content: Use the rule for a power raised to another power: $$ \bigl((xy)^8\bigr)^{\frac{1}{5}}=(xy)^{\frac{8}{5}} $$ The answer is B.
